title: Magical Snappy Korean Zucchini Jeon
description: A charred and snappy Korean dish featuring light rum, namul-blanch-season, and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients, served in individual mini cocottes to cure hangovers. This jeon is a twist on traditional Korean cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together sesame oil, light rum, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and namul seasoning.
Add the sliced zucchinis to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Let the zucchinis marinate for at least 10 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, salt, and black pepper.
Add the egg mixture to the zucchinis and toss to coat.
Divide the zucchini mixture among 4 individual mini cocottes.
Bake the jeon in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until the edges are charred and the centers are set.
Remove the jeon from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
